---
title: "20250530_tscan_trajectories"
author: "Stephanie Hickey"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    code_folding: hide
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      toc_collapsed: true
params:
    project_dir: /mnt/ufs18/rs-013/bioinformaticsCore/projects/chong_davidson/BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA
    project_name: BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA
    lab: Weiming Li
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
## parameterized text
cat("\n")
cat("#### Lab:", params$lab, " \n")
cat("#### Project:", params$project_name, " \n")
```

```{r}
# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scater)
library(scran)
library(patchwork)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(readxl)
library(monocle3)
library(batchelor)
library(BiocSingular)
library(DT)
library(TSCAN)
library(rtracklayer)
source(paste0(params$project_dir, "/src/scRNA_seq_functions.R"))
```

```{r}
# Set Paths
results_dir = paste0(params$project_dir, "/results/20251203")
# results_dir = "/mnt/ufs18/rs-013/bioinformaticsCore/projects/chong_davidson/BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA/results/20251203"
data_dir = paste0(params$project_dir, "/data")
# data_dir = "/mnt/ufs18/rs-013/bioinformaticsCore/projects/chong_davidson/BCC125_sea_lamprey_scRNA/data"
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# load for rendering
all.sce = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/all.sce.Rds"))
merged.sce = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/merged.sce.Rds"))
merged.cds = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/merged.cds.Rds"))
combined.sce = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/combined.sce.Rds"))
```

# Collect individual samples into one object
```{r, eval=FALSE}
# Load QC'd data
sample_dirs = list.dirs(data_dir, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE)
sample_names = basename(sample_dirs)

cds_list = list()

for(i in 1:length(sample_dirs)){
  
  cds_list[[i]] = readRDS(paste0(sample_dirs[i], 
                                 "/", 
                                 sample_names[i],
                                 "_cds_mito_filter.Rds"))
}

names(cds_list) = sample_names
all.sce = list()

# cds to sce
for (cds in names(cds_list)){
  
  raw_counts = counts(cds_list[[cds]])
  all.sce[[cds]] = 
    SingleCellExperiment(list(counts=raw_counts), 
                         colData=cds_list[[cds]]@colData)
  all.sce[[cds]]@colData$sample = cds
  
}

# https://bioconductor.org/books/3.14/OSCA.multisample/human-pbmcs-10x-genomics.html
# normalization, feature selection, dim reduction
all.sce <- lapply(all.sce, logNormCounts)
all.dec <- lapply(all.sce, modelGeneVar)
all.hvgs <- lapply(all.dec, getTopHVGs, prop=0.1)
all.sce <- mapply(FUN=runPCA, x=all.sce, subset_row=all.hvgs)

saveRDS(all.sce, file=paste0(results_dir, "/all.sce.Rds"))
```

# Make info per sample table 

```{r}
df_list = list()

for(samp in names(all.sce)){

  # number of cells
  ncells = ncol(all.sce[[samp]])
  # mean counts
  med_counts = round(median(colData(all.sce[[samp]])$sum))
  # mean genes detected
  med_genes = round(median(colData(all.sce[[samp]])$detected))
  # mean percent mitochondria 
  med_mito = median(colData(all.sce[[samp]])$subsets_Mito_percent)
  med_mito = round(med_mito, digits = 2)
  
  df_list[[samp]] = 
    data.frame(Sample = samp,
              nCells = ncells,
              medianCounts = med_counts,
              medianGenesDetected = med_genes,
              medianPercentMitoReads = med_mito
              ) 
}

df_all = do.call(rbind,df_list)
write.csv(df_all, file = paste0(results_dir, "/median_stats.csv"))

datatable(df_all, rownames = FALSE)
```

# Merge samples, removing sample batch effect

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# integration
# Intersecting the common genes.
universe <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(all.sce, rownames))
all.sce2 <- lapply(all.sce, "[", i=universe,)
all.dec2 <- lapply(all.dec, "[", i=universe,)

# Renormalizing to adjust for differences in depth.
normed.sce <- do.call(multiBatchNorm, all.sce2)

# Identifying a set of HVGs using stats from all batches.
combined.dec <- do.call(combineVar, all.dec2)
combined.hvg <- getTopHVGs(combined.dec, n=5000)

set.seed(1)
merged.sce <- do.call(fastMNN, c(normed.sce, 
    list(subset.row=combined.hvg, BSPARAM=RandomParam())))

# clustering in corrected space
g <- buildSNNGraph(merged.sce, use.dimred="corrected", k=30)
set.seed(1)
colLabels(merged.sce) <- factor(igraph::cluster_louvain(g)$membership)

set.seed(1)
merged.sce <- runUMAP(merged.sce, dimred="corrected")
set.seed(1)
merged.sce <- runTSNE(merged.sce, dimred="corrected")

saveRDS(merged.sce, file = paste0(results_dir, "/merged.sce.Rds"))
```

The plot below shows the merged data in TSNE space separated by sample.

```{r, fig.width=4, fig.height=4}
# plot TSNE

p <- lapply(unique(merged.sce$batch), function(f) {
  sce.f = merged.sce[, which(merged.sce$batch == f)]
  plotTSNE(sce.f, point_size = .05) + ggtitle(f)
})

wrap_plots(p, ncol = 2)
```

This plot shows clusters identified using the "scatter" package in the batch corrected expression space.

```{r, fig.width=6, fig.height=6}
plotTSNE(merged.sce, colour_by="label", text_by="label", text_colour="red", point_size = .05) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=5)))

#ggsave(file = paste0(results_dir, "/all_cells_tsne.png"))
```

This plot shows the proportion of cells from each sample within each new cluster.

```{r}
meta_df = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sce))

sample_tally = 
  meta_df %>%
  group_by(label, batch) %>%
  tally()

ggplot(sample_tally, aes(fill=batch, y=label, x=n)) + 
    geom_bar(position="fill", stat="identity") +
    xlab("Proportion of cells")

```

## Plot known cell-type markers

In each cell, the expression of genes within each group are summed. In the plots, higher expression is a brighter color. Expression is scaled to percent of maximum expression.

Use known markers unique to each cell type:

```{r}
# load known marker genes
library(readr)
chung_davidson_cell_type_markers <- read_csv(paste0(data_dir, "/chung_davidson_cell_type_markers.csv"))
chung_davidson_cell_type_markers = 
  chung_davidson_cell_type_markers %>%
  select(-GeneName)

chung_davidson_unique = 
  chung_davidson_cell_type_markers %>%
  group_by(Gene) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n == 1) %>%
  select(Gene) %>%
  left_join(chung_davidson_cell_type_markers)

datatable(chung_davidson_unique, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```
```{r, eval=F}

# make cds for plotting with monocle
# Intersecting the common genes.
all.dec <- lapply(all.sce, modelGeneVar)
universe <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(all.sce, rownames))
all.sce2 <- lapply(all.sce, "[", i=universe,)
all.dec2 <- lapply(all.dec, "[", i=universe,)

all.sce2[["TM_C00004374"]]@colData$cluster="noclust" 

# uncorrected gene expression
combined.sce <- do.call(cbind, all.sce2)
saveRDS(combined.sce, file = paste0(results_dir, "/combined.sce.Rds"))

merged.cds <- new_cell_data_set(counts(combined.sce),
                                cell_metadata = merged.sce@colData)

reducedDims(merged.cds)$UMAP <- reducedDims(merged.sce)$UMAP
reducedDims(merged.cds)$tSNE <- reducedDims(merged.sce)$TSNE
rowData(merged.cds)$gene_short_name = row.names(merged.cds)

# saveRDS(merged.cds, file = paste0(results_dir, "/merged.cds.Rds"))

```

Plot for early cell markers.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}

plot_cells(merged.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE",
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>% 
             filter(Group %in% c("Germline cells during epigenetic reprogramming", 
                                 "Germline stem cells",
                                 "Embryonic stem cells",
                                 "Germline progenitor and amnionic cells",
                                 "Primordial Germ Cells (PGC",
                                 "Migrating germ cells",
                                 "Late PGC (meiotic entry genes")))
```

Female cell markers

```{r}

plot_cells(merged.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE",
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>% 
             filter(Group %in% c("Female Germ cell", 
                                 "Mitotic oocyte",
                                 "Granulosa cell",
                                 "Oocyte")),
           show_trajectory_graph=FALSE,
           label_cell_groups=FALSE,
           label_leaves=FALSE,
           label_branch_points = FALSE)
```

Male cell markers.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}

plot_cells(merged.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE",
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>% 
             filter(Group %in% c("Male germ cells", 
                                 "Sertoli cell",
                                 "Mitotic Spermatogonia",
                                 "Meiotic Spermatogonia",
                                 "Spermatogonia",
                                 "Primary spermatocyte")),
           show_trajectory_graph=FALSE,
           label_cell_groups=FALSE,
           label_leaves=FALSE,
           label_branch_points = FALSE)
```

## Gene descriptions from Wang et al. and the kPetMar1.pri gtf

Summarized from [Wang et al, 2024](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-55019-x#Sec16):

The sea lamprey reference genome has over 60% of genes labeled with generic "LOC" numbers, meaning their functions are unknown. To enable proper cell type identification using known retina markers, the researchers re-annotated these genes by finding their human equivalents through the following process:
Method:

  1. Converted LOC IDs to protein sequences
  2. Used BLAST to find matching human proteins
  3. Assigned human gene symbols to lamprey genes with human matches (written in UPPERCASE)
  4. For genes without human matches, searched other species (written in lowercase)
  5. Added suffixes to distinguish genes encoding the same protein

```{r}
library(readxl)
# https://static-content.springer.com/esm/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-55019-x/MediaObjects/41467_2024_55019_MOESM3_ESM.xlsx
gene_description = 
  read_excel(paste0(data_dir, "/41467_2024_55019_MOESM3_ESM.xlsx"), skip=1) %>%
  dplyr::rename(WangGeneID = "CountMatrix_GeneID",
                Gene = "NCBI_GTF_GENEID",
                description = "NCBI_GTF_ProteinInfo") %>%
  dplyr::select(Gene,WangGeneID,description) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Gene)) %>%
  filter(!WangGeneID %in% c("LOC116945425.1", "S100A1.1")) # loc has two wang ids


gtf = as.data.frame(readGFF("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_genomic.gtf"))

gtf_filt = 
  gtf %>%
  filter(gbkey %in% c("mRNA", "misc_RNA", "ncRNA", "tRNA", "rRNA")) %>%
  mutate(product = gsub(", transcript variant.*", "", product)) %>%
  select(gene_id, product) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  dplyr::rename(Gene = "gene_id",
                description = "product") %>%
  mutate(WangGeneID = Gene)
 

notWang = gtf_filt$Gene[!gtf_filt$Gene %in% gene_description$Gene]

gtf_add = 
  gtf_filt %>%
  filter(Gene %in% notWang)

gene_description = 
  rbind(gene_description, gtf_add)

write.csv(gene_description, file = paste0(results_dir, "/gene_descritption.csv"), row.names = F)

datatable(gene_description)
```

## Compare scatter cluster enriched genes with known markers

### Cluster enriched genes

This table contains the top ~100 cluster enriched genes for each cluster. All of the cluster enriched genes are saved in `results/scatter_cluster_marker_genes.csv`. The markers can help us map a cluster to a cell type.

In this case, genes in each cluster were compared to genes in every other cluster in a pairwise manner, and the p.values and logFCs of those comparisons have been summarized for ease of interpretation. 

**Column descriptions**

* `cluster`: The cluster corresponding to the enriched genes.      
* `Gene`: The enriched gene   
* `p.value`: The combined p-value across all pairwise comparisons.     
* `FDR`: The BH-adjusted p-value for each gene.   
* `summary.logFC`: This contains the summary effect size, obtained by combining effect sizes from all pairwise comparison into a single value.    

```{r, eval=TRUE}
scatter_markers = findMarkers(combined.sce,
                              merged.sce$label,
                              test.type="t",
                              block=combined.sce$sample,
                              direction="up",
                              pval.type="some")

saveRDS(scatter_markers, file= paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_markers.Rds"))

scatter_summary = list()

for (i in 1:length(scatter_markers)){
  
scatter_markers[[i]]$cluster = i
scatter_markers[[i]]$Gene = rownames(scatter_markers[[i]])
scatter_summary[[i]] = 
  as.data.frame(scatter_markers[[i]]) %>%
  select(cluster, Gene, p.value, FDR, summary.logFC)

}

scatter_markers_df =
  do.call(rbind, scatter_summary) %>%
  left_join(gene_description, by="Gene") %>%
  filter(FDR < .01) 

write.csv(scatter_markers_df, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_genes.csv"))
  
scatter_markers_top100 = 
  scatter_markers_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_min(FDR, n = 100, with_ties=TRUE)
  
datatable(scatter_markers_top100, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)  
```

### Comparison with known markers

We'll check for the significance of the overlap between the top 100 cluster enriched genes and the supplied known cell-type markers. In the plot below, the rows represent cell types with know marker genes, and the columns represent the cell clusters. The number in each box is the number of genes shared between the top 100 cluster enriched genes for the specified clusters and the specified known cell-type markers. Each box is colored by the significance of the overlap ($-log10(FDR)$). Significant overlaps may signify that the cells in the cluster belong to the specified cell type. 

* Overlap p-values are save in `results/scatter_cluster_marker_overlap_pvalues.csv`.    
* Overlaps between cluster enriched genes and known marker genes are save in `results/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_overlaps.csv`.    

```{r, fig.width=8}
marker_test_genes =
  scatter_markers_top100 %>%
  select(Gene, cluster) %>%
  mutate(cluster = paste0("cluster", cluster))

cluster_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(chung_davidson_cell_type_markers, 
                                marker_test_genes, length(merged.sce))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  rename(Gene = "SharedGene")

cluster_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  cluster_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(cluster_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = cluster_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  rename(Group1 = "CellTypes",
         Group2 = "Clusters") %>%
  group_by(CellTypes) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH"))

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_overlap_pvalues.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores$FDR[scores$FDR > .01] = 1

FDR_wide <- 
  scores %>%
  mutate(logFDR = -log10(FDR)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(id_cols = CellTypes, 
                     names_from = Clusters, 
                     values_from=logFDR)

CellTypes = FDR_wide$CellTypes
FDR_wide$CellTypes = NULL

FDR_wide = as.matrix(FDR_wide)
rownames(FDR_wide) = CellTypes

ord_row <- hclust( dist(FDR_wide), method = "ward.D" )$order
ord_col <- hclust( dist(t(FDR_wide)), method = "ward.D" )$order

scores$CellTypes = factor(scores$CellTypes, levels = rownames(FDR_wide)[ord_row])
scores$Clusters = factor(scores$Clusters, levels = colnames(FDR_wide)[ord_col])

plot1 =
    ggplot(scores,
           aes(Clusters, CellTypes)) + 
           geom_tile(aes(fill = -log10(FDR)), 
                     color = "light gray") +
        #Creating legend
        guides(fill=guide_colorbar(title = "-log10(FDR)",
                                   barheight = 10,
                                   barwidth = 2,
                                   default.unit = "mm")) +
        #Creating color range
        scale_fill_gradientn(colors=c("white",
                                      "#fd9668",
                                      "#f1605d",
                                      "#de4968",
                                      "#9e2f7f"),
                             guide="colorbar",
                             limits = c(0, ceiling(-log10(min(scores$FDR))))
                             ) +
        #Rotating labels
         theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, 
                                          hjust = 1,vjust=1),
               ) +
        theme(text = element_text(size = 7),
              axis.text = element_text(size = 7)) +
        geom_text(aes(label = nSharedGenes), size = 1) +
        coord_fixed()

plot1

ggsave(plot1, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_overlap_heatmap.png"))
```

## Gene ontology of top 100 scatter cluster enriched genes 

To help annotate cell type clusters, we can check for enriched gene ontology categories among cell-type enriched genes.

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Clusters`: Cluster   
* `nClusterEnrichedGenes`: Number of cluster enriched genes      
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and cluster enriched genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons         

Overlap p-values are save in `results/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_overlap_pvalues.csv`. Overlaps between cluster enriched genes and known marker genes are save in `results/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_overlaps.csv`.

```{r}
gaf = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_gene_ontology.gaf", header=F, comment = "!")

gaf <- 
  gaf %>%
  filter(!grepl("NOT", V4)) %>%  
  dplyr::select(V3, V5) %>%      
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "V5",            
                Gene = "V3") %>%
  distinct() 

keep_go = 
  gaf %>%
  group_by(GOID) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n > 10,
         n < 200) %>%
  pull(GOID)

gaf = 
  gaf %>%
  filter(GOID %in% keep_go)

go_names = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GOs_names.txt", header = F)
colnames(go_names) = c("GOID", "GOName")
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           marker_test_genes, length(merged.cds))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  rename(Gene = "SharedGene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Clusters = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nClusterEnrichedGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
  group_by(Clusters) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(Pval < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_overlap_pvalues.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Annotations

| cluster | annotation                        |
| ------- | --------------------------------- |
| 1       | Fibroblasts                       |
| 2       | Mesonephric cells                 |
| 3       | Pre-granulosa cells I             |
| 4       | Granulosa cells                   |
| 5       | Primordial germ cells             |
| 6       | Endothelial cells                 |
| 7       | Migrating male germ cells         |
| 8       | Erythrocytes                      |
| 9       | Pre-Sertoli cells                 |
| 10      | Mast cells                        |
| 11      | Hemopoietic stem cells            |
| 12      | Podocytes                         |
| 13      | Macrophages                       |
| 14      | Pre-granulosa cells II            |
| 15      | Migrating immune cells            |
| 16      | Spermatogonia stem cells          |
| 17      | Spermatogonia A                   |
| 18      | Spermatocyte                      |
| 19      | Spermatogonia B                   |

# Sex cell clusters analysis 

Filter for cells in clusters 16, 17, 18, 19, 5, 7 

| sex clusters | annotation                   |
| ------------ | ---------------------------- |
| 7            | Migrating male germ cells    |
| 5            | Primordial germ cells        |
| 16           | Spermatogonia stem cells     |
| 17           | Spermatogonia A              |
| 18           | Spermatocyte                 |
| 19           | Spermatogonia B              |

```{r, echo=FALSE}
# load for rendering
all.sex.sce = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.sex.sce.Rds"))
merged.sex.sce = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.merged.sex.sce.Rds"))
merged.sex.cds = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.merged.sex.cds.Rds"))
```

```{r, eval=F}
# Load QC'd data
sample_dirs = list.dirs(data_dir, full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE)
sample_names = basename(sample_dirs)

cds_list = list()

for(i in 1:length(sample_dirs)){
  
  cds_list[[i]] = readRDS(paste0(sample_dirs[i], 
                                 "/", 
                                 sample_names[i],
                                 "_cds_mito_filter.Rds"))
}

names(cds_list) = sample_names

meta_df = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sce))
meta_df$cells = rownames(meta_df)

all.sex.sce = list()

# cds to sce
for (cds in names(cds_list)){
  
  raw_counts = counts(cds_list[[cds]])
  
  all.sce = 
    SingleCellExperiment(list(counts=raw_counts), 
                         colData=cds_list[[cds]]@colData)
  
  all.sce@colData$sample = cds
  
  sex.cells = 
   meta_df %>%
   filter(label %in% c(16, 17, 18, 19, 5, 7),
          batch == cds) %>%
   pull(cells)
  
  all.sex.sce[[cds]] = all.sce[,sex.cells]
}

# https://bioconductor.org/books/3.14/OSCA.multisample/human-pbmcs-10x-genomics.html
# normalization, feature selection, dim reduction
set.seed(1)
all.sex.sce <- lapply(all.sex.sce, logNormCounts)
all.sex.dec <- lapply(all.sex.sce, modelGeneVar)
all.sex.hvgs <- lapply(all.sex.dec, getTopHVGs, prop=0.1)
all.sex.sce <- mapply(FUN=runPCA, x=all.sex.sce, subset_row=all.sex.hvgs)

saveRDS(all.sex.sce, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.sex.sce.Rds"))
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
all.sex.dec <- lapply(all.sex.sce, modelGeneVar)

# integration
# Intersecting the common genes.
universe <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(all.sex.sce, rownames))
all.sex.sce2 <- lapply(all.sex.sce, "[", i=universe,)
all.sex.dec2 <- lapply(all.sex.dec, "[", i=universe,)

# Renormalizing to adjust for differences in depth.
normed.sce <- do.call(multiBatchNorm, all.sex.sce2)

# Identifying a set of HVGs using stats from all batches.
combined.dec <- do.call(combineVar, all.sex.dec2)
combined.hvg <- getTopHVGs(combined.dec, n=5000)
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
set.seed(1)
merged.sex.sce <- do.call(fastMNN, c(normed.sce, 
    list(subset.row=combined.hvg, BSPARAM=RandomParam())))

# clustering in corrected space
set.seed(1)
#g <- buildSNNGraph(merged.sex.sce, use.dimred="corrected", k = 8)
g <- buildSNNGraph(merged.sex.sce, use.dimred="corrected", k = 20)
set.seed(1)
colLabels(merged.sex.sce) <- factor(igraph::cluster_louvain(g)$membership)

# add all cells scatter clusters
all.scatter.clust = 
  meta_df %>%
  filter(label %in% c(16, 17, 18, 19, 5, 7)) %>%
  select(cells, label) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

all.scatter.clust <- all.scatter.clust[colnames(merged.sex.sce)]
merged.sex.sce$all.scatter.clust = all.scatter.clust

merged.sex.sce <- runUMAP(merged.sex.sce, dimred="corrected")
merged.sex.sce <- runTSNE(merged.sex.sce, dimred="corrected")

saveRDS(merged.sex.sce, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.merged.sex.sce.Rds"))
```

## Plot new clusters in TSNE space

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=4}
tsne_new = plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce, colour_by="label", text_by="label", text_colour="black", point_size = .05)
tsne_old = plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce, colour_by="all.scatter.clust", text_by="all.scatter.clust", text_colour="black", point_size = .05)
tsne_new + tsne_old
```

### Table of original to new cluster assignment
Below is a table showing how many cells from each original sex cell cluster went to each new sex cell cluster.

```{r}

df <- as.data.frame(merged.sex.sce@colData)

df_count <- 
  df %>%
  select(label, all.scatter.clust) %>%
  dplyr::rename(sex.merged.clusters = "label") %>%
  dplyr::count(sex.merged.clusters, all.scatter.clust) %>%
  group_by(all.scatter.clust) %>%
  mutate(total_m1 = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(sex.merged.clusters) %>%
  mutate(total_m2 = sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Compute the percentage of total in method 1 and method 2
  mutate(
    pct_of_all.clusters = round((n / total_m1) * 100, 2),
    pct_of_sex.clusters = round((n / total_m2) * 100, 2)) %>%
  select(-total_m1, -total_m2)
  
datatable(df_count, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Sex subclusters mapping to cluster annotations

| sex subclusters | annotation                    |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| 1               | Primordial germ cells         |
| 2               | Migrating male germ cells     |
| 3               | Migrating male germ cells     |
| 4               | Primordial germ cells         |
| 5               | Migrating male germ cells     |
| 6               | Primordial germ cells         |
| 7               | Spermatogonia stem cells      |
| 8               | Spermatogonia A               |
| 9               | Spermatocyte                  |
| 10              | Spermatogonia B               |
| 11              | Spermatogonia A/Spermatocyte  |
| 12              | Spermatogonia A/Spermatocyte  |
| 13              | Spermatogonia B               |
| 14              | Spermatogonia A               |
| 15              | Spermatogonia/Spermatocyte    |

## Split by sample

```{r,fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
# plot TSNE

p <- lapply(unique(merged.sex.sce$batch), function(f) {
  sce.f = merged.sex.sce[, which(merged.sex.sce$batch == f)]
  plotTSNE(sce.f, colour_by="label", text_by="label", text_colour="black", point_size = .05) + ggtitle(f)
})

wrap_plots(p, ncol = 2)
```

This plot shows the proportion of cells from each sample within each new cluster.

```{r}
meta_df = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce))

sample_tally = 
  meta_df %>%
  mutate(label = as.factor(label),
         batch = as.factor(batch)) %>%
  group_by(label, batch, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  tally()

ggplot(sample_tally, aes(fill=batch, y=label, x=n)) + 
    geom_bar(position="fill", stat="identity") +
    xlab("Proportion of cells")
```


```{r, eval=TRUE}
# make cds for plotting with monocle
all.sex.dec <- lapply(all.sex.sce, modelGeneVar)
# integration
# Intersecting the common genes.
universe <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(all.sex.sce, rownames))
all.sex.sce2 <- lapply(all.sex.sce, "[", i=universe,)
all.sex.dec2 <- lapply(all.sex.dec, "[", i=universe,)

all.sex.sce2[["TM_C00004374"]]@colData$cluster="noclust" 

combined.sex.sce <- do.call(cbind, all.sex.sce2)
```
```{r, eval=FALSE}
merged.sex.cds <- new_cell_data_set(counts(combined.sex.sce),
                                cell_metadata = merged.sex.sce@colData)

reducedDims(merged.sex.cds)$tSNE <- reducedDims(merged.sex.sce)$TSNE
rowData(merged.sex.cds)$gene_short_name = row.names(merged.sex.cds)

saveRDS(merged.sex.cds, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support.merged.sex.cds.Rds"))
```

## Plot known markers

Plot for early cell markers.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
plot_cells(merged.sex.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE", 
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>% 
             filter(Group %in% c("Germline cells during epigenetic reprogramming", 
                                 "Germline stem cells",
                                 "Embryonic stem cells",
                                 "Germline progenitor and amnionic cells",
                                 "Primordial Germ Cells (PGC",
                                 "Migrating germ cells",
                                 "Late PGC (meiotic entry genes")))
```

Female cell markers

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=6}
plot_cells(merged.sex.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE", 
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>% 
             filter(Group %in% c("Female Germ cell", 
                                 "Mitotic oocyte",
                                 "Granulosa cell",
                                 "Oocyte")),
           show_trajectory_graph=FALSE,
           label_cell_groups=FALSE,
           label_leaves=FALSE,
           label_branch_points = FALSE)
```

Male cell markers.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
plot_cells(merged.sex.cds, reduction_method = "tSNE", 
           genes=chung_davidson_unique %>%  
             filter(Group %in% c("Male germ cells", 
                                 "Sertoli cell",
                                 "Mitotic Spermatogonia",
                                 "Meiotic Spermatogonia",
                                 "Spermatogonia",
                                 "Primary spermatocyte")),
           show_trajectory_graph=FALSE,
           label_cell_groups=FALSE,
           label_leaves=FALSE,
           label_branch_points = FALSE)
```

## Cluster enriched genes

This table contains the top ~100 cluster enriched genes for each cluster. All of the cluster enriched genes are saved in `sex_scatter_cluster_marker_genes.csv`. The markers can help us map a cluster to a cell type.

In this case, genes in each cluster were compared to genes in every other cluster in a pairwise manner, and the p.values and logFCs of those comparisons have been summarized for ease of interpretation. 

**Column descriptions**

* `cluster`: The cluster corresponding to the enriched genes.      
* `Gene`: The enriched gene   
* `p.value`: The combined p-value across all pairwise comparisons.     
* `FDR`: The BH-adjusted p-value for each gene.   
* `summary.logFC`: This contains the summary effect size, obtained by combining effect sizes from all pairwise comparison into a single value.   
* `WangGeneID`: BLAST equivalent gene name dervived by Wang et al, 2024   
* `description`: Gene description   

```{r, eval=FALSE}
scatter_markers = findMarkers(combined.sex.sce,
                              merged.sex.sce$label,
                              test.type="t",
                              block=combined.sex.sce$sample,
                              direction="up",
                              pval.type="some")

saveRDS(scatter_markers, file= paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_markers.Rds"))
```

```{r}
scatter_markers = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_markers.Rds"))

scatter_summary = list()

for (i in 1:length(scatter_markers)){
  
scatter_markers[[i]]$cluster = i
scatter_markers[[i]]$Gene = rownames(scatter_markers[[i]])
scatter_summary[[i]] = 
  as.data.frame(scatter_markers[[i]]) %>%
  select(cluster, Gene, p.value, FDR, summary.logFC)

}

scatter_markers_df =
  do.call(rbind, scatter_summary) %>%
  left_join(gene_description, by="Gene") %>%
  mutate(cluster = paste0("cluster_",cluster)) %>%
  filter(FDR < .01)

write.csv(scatter_markers_df, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_cluster_marker_genes.csv"))
  
scatter_markers_top100 = 
  scatter_markers_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_min(FDR, n = 100, with_ties=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(p.value = formatC(signif(p.value, digits = 3)),
         FDR = formatC(signif(FDR, digits = 3)), 
         summary.logFC = round(summary.logFC, digits = 3))
  
datatable(scatter_markers_top100, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)  
```

## Comparison with known markers

We'll check for the significance of the overlap between the top 100 cluster enriched genes and the supplied known cell-type markers. In the plot below, the rows represent cell types with know marker genes, and the columns represent the cell clusters. The number in each box is the number of genes shared between the top 100 cluster enriched genes for the specified clusters and the specified known cell-type markers. Each box is colored by the significance of the overlap ($-log10(FDR)$). Significant overlaps may signify that the cells in the cluster belong to the specified cell type. 

```{r, fig.width=8}
marker_test_genes =
  scatter_markers_top100 %>%
  select(Gene, cluster) 

cluster_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(chung_davidson_cell_type_markers, 
                                marker_test_genes, length(merged.sex.sce))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SharedGene = "Gene")

cluster_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  cluster_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(cluster_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_cluster_marker_gene_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = cluster_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(CellTypes = "Group1",
         Clusters = "Group2") %>%
  group_by(CellTypes) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH"))

#scores$FDR[scores$FDR > .01] = 1

FDR_wide <- 
  scores %>%
  mutate(logFDR = -log10(FDR)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(id_cols = CellTypes, 
                    names_from = Clusters, 
                    values_from=logFDR)

CellTypes = FDR_wide$CellTypes
FDR_wide$CellTypes = NULL

FDR_wide = as.matrix(FDR_wide)
rownames(FDR_wide) = CellTypes

ord_row <- hclust( dist(FDR_wide), method = "ward.D" )$order
ord_col <- hclust( dist(t(FDR_wide)), method = "ward.D" )$order

scores$CellTypes = factor(scores$CellTypes, levels = rownames(FDR_wide)[ord_row])
scores$Clusters = factor(scores$Clusters, levels = colnames(FDR_wide)[ord_col])

plot1 =
    ggplot(scores,
           aes(Clusters, CellTypes)) + 
           geom_tile(aes(fill = -log10(FDR)), 
                     color = "light gray") +
        #Creating legend
        guides(fill=guide_colorbar(title = "-log10(FDR)",
                                   barheight = 10,
                                   barwidth = 2,
                                   default.unit = "mm")) +
        #Creating color range
        scale_fill_gradientn(colors=c("white",
                                      "#fd9668",
                                      "#f1605d",
                                      "#de4968",
                                      "#9e2f7f"),
                             guide="colorbar",
                             limits = c(0, ceiling(-log10(min(scores$FDR))))
                             ) +
        #Rotating labels
         theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, 
                                          hjust = 1,vjust=1),
               ) +
        theme(text = element_text(size = 7),
              axis.text = element_text(size = 7)) +
        geom_text(aes(label = nSharedGenes), size = 1) +
        coord_fixed()

plot1

ggsave(plot1, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_cluster_overlap_heatmap.png"))
```

## Gene ontology of top 100 scatter cluster enriched genes 

To help annotate cell type clusters, we can check for enriched gene ontology categories among cell-type enriched genes.

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Clusters`: Cluster   
* `nClusterEnrichedGenes`: Number of cluster enriched genes      
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and cluster enriched genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons            

```{r}
gaf = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_gene_ontology.gaf", header=F, comment = "!")

gaf <- 
  gaf %>%
  filter(!grepl("NOT", V4)) %>%  
  dplyr::select(V3, V5) %>%      
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "V5",            
                Gene = "V3") %>%
  distinct() 

keep_go = 
  gaf %>%
  group_by(GOID) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n > 10,
         n < 200) %>%
  pull(GOID)

gaf = 
  gaf %>%
  filter(GOID %in% keep_go)

go_names = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GOs_names.txt", header = F)
colnames(go_names) = c("GOID", "GOName")
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           marker_test_genes, length(merged.sex.sce))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SharedGene = "Gene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Clusters = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nClusterEnrichedGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
  group_by(Clusters) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(nSharedGenes > 0)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_scatter_cluster_marker_gene_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

# Sex cell developement trajectory analysis

## Trajectory plotted in TSNE space

```{r}
set.seed(1)
by.cluster <- aggregateAcrossCells(merged.sex.sce, 
                                   ids=colLabels(merged.sex.sce), use.assay.type = "reconstructed")

centroids <- reducedDim(by.cluster, "corrected")

mst <- createClusterMST(centroids, clusters=NULL)

line.data <- reportEdges(by.cluster, mst=mst, clusters=NULL, use.dimred="TSNE")

plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce, colour_by="label", point_size = .5) + 
    geom_line(data=line.data, mapping=aes(x=TSNE1, y=TSNE2, group=edge)) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=5)))
```

## Trajectory plotted in batch corrected gene expression space

It's hard to interpret this in TSNE space, so we'll plot in batch corrected expression space instead because the clusters and trajectory analysis were actually calculated in batch corrected expression space. 

```{r}
line.data_corrected <- reportEdges(by.cluster, mst=mst, clusters=NULL, use.dimred="corrected")

plotReducedDim(merged.sex.sce, colour_by="label", dimred = "corrected", point_size = .4) + 
    geom_line(data=line.data_corrected, mapping=aes(x=dim1, y=dim2, group=edge)) + guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=5)))
```

```{r, eval=TRUE}
set.seed(1)
pseudo <- quickPseudotime(merged.sex.sce, use.dimred="corrected", outgroup=FALSE, start = 1)
saveRDS(pseudo, file=paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_pseudo.Rds"))
```

## Trajectory plotted with each cluster as a node

Simplify even further. 

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
pseudo = readRDS(paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_pseudo.Rds"))

library(ggforce)
library(ggraph)
library(tidygraph)
library(scatterpie)
library(ggrepel)

gr <- as_tbl_graph(pseudo$mst)

node_meta =
  sample_tally %>%
  mutate(batch = case_when(
    batch == "TF_C00004337" ~ "Female",
    batch == "TM_C00004374" ~ "Male",
    batch == "UND1_C00004336" ~ "Undifferentiated.1",
    batch == "UND2_C00004362" ~ "Undifferentiated.2"
  )) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = batch, values_from = n) %>%
  mutate(totalCells = sum(Female, 
                          Male, 
                          Undifferentiated.1, 
                          Undifferentiated.2, 
                          na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(name = "label")

group_vec =
  c("primordial GC",
    "migrating male GC",
    "migrating male GC",
    "oogonia",
    "migrating male GC",
    "oogonia",
    "spermatogonia stem cells",
    "spermatogonia A",
    "spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia B",
    "spermatogonia A/spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia A/spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia B",
    "spermatogonia A",
    "spermatogonia AB/spermatocyte"
    )

node_meta$CellType = group_vec

gr = 
  gr %>% 
  activate(nodes) %>%
  left_join(node_meta) %>%
  arrange(desc(totalCells))


layout_data <- create_layout(gr, layout = 'stress')

hull_data <- layout_data %>%
  group_by(CellType) %>%
  slice(chull(x, y))

hull_centroids = 
  hull_data %>%
  group_by(CellType) %>%
  summarise(hull_x = mean(x),
            hull_y = mean(y))

label_positions <- tibble(
  CellType = c(
    "migrating male GC",
    "oogonia",
    "primordial GC",
    "spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia A",
    "spermatogonia A/spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia AB/spermatocyte",
    "spermatogonia B",
    "spermatogonia stem cells"
  ),
  label_x = c(
    -0.15,   # migrating male GC - move right below point 3
    0.348,   # oogonia - keep upper right
    -0.1,   # primordial GC - move slightly right
    -0.60,   # spermatocyte - move right to avoid overlap
    -1.15,   # spermatogonia A - move left of cluster
    -0.82,   # spermatogonia A/spermatocyte - below cluster
    -1.12,   # spermatogonia AB/spermatocyte - keep above point 15
    -1.32,   # spermatogonia B - move right to avoid stem cells label
    -1.35    # spermatogonia stem cells - keep left
  ),
  label_y = c(
    -0.35,   # migrating male GC - below point 3
    -0.12,   # oogonia - above cluster
    -0.20,   # primordial GC - at current height
    -0.60,   # spermatocyte - below point 9
    -0.30,   # spermatogonia A - left of points 8, 14
    -0.50,   # spermatogonia A/spermatocyte - below points 11, 12
    0.25,   # spermatogonia AB/spermatocyte - above point 15
    0.10,   # spermatogonia B - between clusters
    -0.35    # spermatogonia stem cells - below point 7
  )
)

label_positions = left_join(label_positions, hull_centroids)

gr_plot =
  ggraph(gr, "stress") + 
  geom_shape(
    data = hull_data, 
    aes(x, y, 
        fill = CellType, 
        fill = after_scale("gray"),
        group = CellType),
    expand = 0.04,
    radius = 0.04,
    show.legend = FALSE,
    alpha = .2
  ) +
  # Hulls mapped to CellType, but all gray + no legend
  geom_segment(data = label_positions,
               aes(x = hull_x, y = hull_y,
                   xend = label_x, yend = label_y),
               color = "gray",
               linetype="dashed",
               size = 0.5,
               arrow = NULL) +
  geom_edge_link() +
  # Scatter pies with manual color scale + legend
  geom_label(data = label_positions,
             aes(x = label_x, y = label_y, label = CellType),
             size = 3,
             label.padding = unit(0.25, "lines")) +
  geom_scatterpie(
    aes(x, y),
    cols = c("Female", "Male", "Undifferentiated.1", "Undifferentiated.2"),
    data = layout_data,
    colour = NA,
    pie_scale = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Sample",
    values = c(
      "Female" = "#FF6DB6",
      "Male" = "#006ddb",
      "Undifferentiated.1" = "#009292",
      "Undifferentiated.2" = "#b66ddf"
    )
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(keywidth = .8,
                             keyheight =.8)) + 
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), color = "black") +
  theme_void() + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "left",
    legend.justification = "center",  # left-align content inside legend box
    legend.box.just = "center"           # align legend box in layout
  )

gr_plot + coord_fixed()

```

## Cluster 7 vs cluster 13 differential expression
```{r}
keep = 
  as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce)) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "barcode") %>%
  filter(label %in% c(7,13)) %>%
  pull(barcode)
  
clust7v13 = findMarkers(combined.sex.sce[,keep],
                        merged.sex.sce[,keep]$label,
                        test.type="t",
                        block=combined.sex.sce[,keep]$sample,
                        direction="up",
                        pval.type="some")

clust7v13_summary = list()

for (i in c(7,13)){
  
clust7v13[[i]]$cluster = i
clust7v13[[i]]$Gene = rownames(clust7v13[[i]])
clust7v13_summary[[i]] = 
  as.data.frame(clust7v13[[i]]) %>%
  select(cluster, Gene, p.value, FDR, summary.logFC)

}

clust7v13_df =
  do.call(rbind, clust7v13_summary) %>%
  left_join(gene_description, by="Gene") %>%
  mutate(cluster = paste0("cluster_",cluster)) %>%
  filter(FDR < .01)

write.csv(clust7v13_df, file = paste0(results_dir, "/cluster7vs13_de_genes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(clust7v13_df %>%
            mutate(p.value = formatC(signif(p.value, digits = 3)),
                   FDR = formatC(signif(FDR, digits = 3)), 
                   summary.logFC = round(summary.logFC, digits = 3)),
          filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Gene ontology 
```{r}

clust7v13_genes = 
  clust7v13_df %>%
  select(Gene, cluster)

gaf = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_gene_ontology.gaf", header=F, comment = "!")

gaf <- 
  gaf %>%
  filter(!grepl("NOT", V4)) %>%  
  dplyr::select(V3, V5) %>%      
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "V5",            
                Gene = "V3") %>%
  distinct() 

keep_go = 
  gaf %>%
  group_by(GOID) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n > 10,
         n < 200) %>%
  pull(GOID)

gaf = 
  gaf %>%
  filter(GOID %in% keep_go)

go_names = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GOs_names.txt", header = F)
colnames(go_names) = c("GOID", "GOName")
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           clust7v13_genes, length(merged.sex.cds))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  rename(Gene = "SharedGene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/cluster7vs13_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Clusters = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nClusterEnrichedGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
  group_by(Clusters) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(Pval < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/cluster7vs13_GO_overlap_pvalues.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Clusters 8,13,6,5,9 vs 12,10,11,7 differential expression

```{r}
keep = 
  as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce)) %>%
  rownames_to_column(var = "barcode") %>%
  filter(label %in% c(8,13,6,5,9,12,10,11,7)) %>%
  pull(barcode)

male.sce = combined.sex.sce[,keep]

meta = 
  as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce)) %>%
  filter(label %in% c(8,13,6,5,9,12,10,11,7)) %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    label %in% c(8,13,6,5,9) ~ "group13",
    label %in% c(12,10,11,7) ~ "group7"
  ))

male.sce$Group = meta$Group
  
group7v13 = findMarkers(male.sce,
                        male.sce$Group,
                        test.type="t",
                        block=male.sce$sample,
                        direction="up",
                        pval.type="some")

group7v13_summary = list()

for (g in names(group7v13)){
  
group7v13[[g]]$Group = g
group7v13[[g]]$Gene = rownames(group7v13[[g]])
group7v13_summary[[g]] = 
  as.data.frame(group7v13[[g]]) %>%
  select(Group, Gene, p.value, FDR, summary.logFC)

}

group7v13_df =
  do.call(rbind, group7v13_summary) %>%
  left_join(gene_description, by="Gene") %>%
  filter(FDR < .01)

write.csv(group7v13_df, file = paste0(results_dir, "/group7vs13_de_genes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(group7v13_df %>%
            mutate(p.value = formatC(signif(p.value, digits = 3)),
                   FDR = formatC(signif(FDR, digits = 3)), 
                   summary.logFC = round(summary.logFC, digits = 3)),
          filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```
### Gene ontology
```{r}

group7v13_genes = 
  group7v13_df %>%
  select(Gene, Group)

go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           group7v13_genes, length(merged.sex.cds))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  rename(Gene = "SharedGene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/group7vs13_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Clusters = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nClusterEnrichedGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
  group_by(Clusters) %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(Pval < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/group7vs13_GO_overlap_pvalues.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```
## Plot pseudotime

### In tSNE space

```{r}
common.pseudo <- averagePseudotime(pseudo$ordering) 
merged.sex.sce$Pseudotime <- common.pseudo

pseudo_plot = 
  plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce, colour_by="Pseudotime", 
        text_by="label", text_colour="red",  point_size = .5, text_size = 3) +
    geom_line(data=line.data, mapping=aes(x=TSNE1, y=TSNE2, group=edge))

pseudo_plot
```

### In batch corrected gene expression space

It's hard to interpret this in TSNE space, so we'll plot in batch corrected expression space instead because the clusters and trajectory analysis were actually calculated in batch corrected expression space. 

```{r}
plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce, dimred = "corrected", colour_by=I(common.pseudo), 
        text_by="label", text_colour="red",  point_size = .5) +
     geom_line(data=line.data_corrected, mapping=aes(x=dim1, y=dim2, group=edge))
```

## Genes changing with pseudotime along each path

Top ~100 genes by FDR changing with pseudotime along each path. All of the significant genes are saved in `/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_genes.csv`

**Column descriptions**

* `Path`: The leaf node of the path along which the genes are changing
* `SYMBOL`: The pseudotime-associated gene
*	`WangGeneID`: BLAST equivalent gene name derived by Wang et al, 2024
* `description`: Gene description
* `logFC`: estimated log-fold change of expression along the path
* `p.value`: p-value for each gene.
* `FDR`: The BH-adjusted p-value for each gene.

```{r}
pseudo_mat = pathStat(pseudo$ordering)
test_pseudo <- TSCAN::testPseudotime(combined.sex.sce, 
                              pseudotime=pseudo_mat)

test_pseudo_filt = list()

for (i in 1:length(test_pseudo)){
  
  test_pseudo[[i]]$SYMBOL <- rownames(combined.sex.sce)
  test_pseudo[[i]]$Path <- names(test_pseudo)[i]
  
  test_pseudo_filt[[i]] =
    as.data.frame(test_pseudo[[i]]) %>%
    filter(FDR < .01) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(logFC)))
  
}

test_pseudo_filt_df = do.call(rbind, test_pseudo_filt)

symbol_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SYMBOL = "Gene")

test_pseudo_filt_df = 
  do.call(rbind, test_pseudo_filt) %>%
  left_join(symbol_description, by = "SYMBOL")

test_pseudo_filt_df = test_pseudo_filt_df[, c("Path", "SYMBOL", "WangGeneID", "description", "logFC", "p.value", "FDR")]

write.csv(test_pseudo_filt_df, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_genes.csv"))

test_pseudo100 = 
  test_pseudo_filt_df %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ paste0(Path, ".Upregulated"),
    logFC < 0 ~  paste0(Path, ".Downregulated"))
    ) %>%
  group_by(Path) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=100) %>%
  mutate(logFC = round(logFC, digits = 2),
         p.value = formatC(signif(p.value, digits = 2)),
         FDR = formatC(signif(FDR, digits = 2)))
  
test_pseudo = 
  test_pseudo_filt_df %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ paste0(Path, ".Upregulated"),
    logFC < 0 ~  paste0(Path, ".Downregulated"))
    ) %>%
  group_by(Path) %>%
  mutate(logFC = round(logFC, digits = 2),
         p.value = formatC(signif(p.value, digits = 2)),
         FDR = formatC(signif(FDR, digits = 2)))

datatable(test_pseudo100, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Gene ontology enrichment, top ~100 genes

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Path`: Trajectory path, upregulated or downregulated genes   
* `nPathDEGenes`: Number of pseudotime-associated genes      
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and pseudotime-associated genes    
* `Pval`: p-value of the pseudotime association                   
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons         

Overlap p-values are save in `results/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_top100_GO_pvals.csv`. Overlaps between cluster enriched genes and known marker genes are save in `results/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_top100_GO_overlaps.csv`.

```{r}
test_pseudo100_genes =
  test_pseudo100 %>%
  select(SYMBOL, Path)

go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           test_pseudo100_genes, length(merged.sex.sce))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SharedGene = "Gene")

go_int = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene") %>%
  filter(!SharedGene == "none") %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2") %>%
  left_join(go_names, by="GOID") %>%
  relocate(GOName, .after = GOID)

write.csv(go_int, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_top100_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nPathDEGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(FDR < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_pseudotime_de_top100_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 


datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Genes differentially expressed immediately following the branch point in cluster 14

A more advanced analysis involves looking for differences in expression between paths of a branched trajectory. This is most interesting for cells close to the branch point between two or more paths where the differential expression analysis may highlight genes responsible for the branching event. The general strategy here is to fit one trend to the unique part of each path immediately following the branch point, followed by a comparison of the fits between paths.

These plots show pseudotime in cells in cluster 14 along paths 2 and 8 which separate male and female trajectories. 

### In TSNE space

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=8, eval=T}
set.seed(1)
pseudo14 <- quickPseudotime(merged.sex.sce, use.dimred="corrected", outgroup=FALSE, start = 14)

combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.15 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,1]
combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.8 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,2]
combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.2 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,3]
combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.4 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,4]
combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.9 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,5]
combined.sex.sce$TSCAN.7 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,6]

merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.15 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,1]
merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.8 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,2]
merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.2 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,3]
merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.4 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,4]
merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.9 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,5]
merged.sex.sce$TSCAN.7 <- pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)[,6]

# Subsetting to the desired cluster containing the branch point.
keep <- colLabels(merged.sex.sce) == 14
combined.sex.sce14 <- combined.sex.sce[,keep]
merged.sex.sce14 <- merged.sex.sce[,keep]

# Showing only the lines to/from our cluster of interest.
line.data.sub <- line.data[grepl("^14--", line.data$edge) | grepl("--14$", line.data$edge),]
ggline <- geom_line(data=line.data.sub, mapping=aes(x=TSNE1, y=TSNE2, group=edge))

ggline <- geom_line(data=line.data.sub, mapping=aes(x=TSNE1, y=TSNE2, group=edge)) 

gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce14, colour_by="TSCAN.2", point_size = .3) + ggline,
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce14, colour_by="TSCAN.8", point_size = .3) + ggline,
    ncol=2
)
```

### In batch corrected space 

It's hard to interpret this in TSNE space, so we'll plot in batch corrected expression space instead because the clusters and trajectory analysis were actually calculated in batch corrected expression space. 

```{r, fig.height=2, fig.width=10, eval=TRUE}
line.data.sub_corrected = line.data_corrected[grepl("^14--", line.data_corrected$edge) | grepl("--14$", line.data_corrected$edge),]
line.data.sub_corrected <- line.data.sub_corrected[grepl("--1", line.data.sub_corrected$edge) | grepl("--13", line.data.sub_corrected$edge),]

ggline_corrected <- geom_line(data=line.data.sub_corrected, mapping=aes(x=dim1, y=dim2, group=edge))

ps = pathStat(pseudo14$ordering)

# cells on female path
pathF = rownames(ps)[!is.na(ps[,3])]
# cells on male path
pathM = rownames(ps)[!is.na(ps[,2])]
# cells that haven't yet branched
pathUB = rownames(ps)[!is.na(ps[,1])]

pathF = pathF[!pathF %in% pathUB]
pathM = pathM[!pathM %in% pathUB]

coldat = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce14))
coldat$CellId = rownames(coldat)

coldat = 
  coldat %>%
  mutate(CellPath = case_when(
    CellId %in% pathF ~ "Female",
    CellId %in% pathM ~ "Male",
    CellId %in% pathUB ~ "Unbranched",
  ))

merged.sex.sce14$Branch = coldat$CellPath

path_plot = plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce14, dimred = "corrected", colour_by="Branch", point_size = .5) + ggline_corrected + guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=3)))

merged.sex.sce14$Pseuodtime = coldat$TSCAN.2

fplot = plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce14, dimred = "corrected", colour_by="Pseuodtime", point_size = .5) + ggline_corrected 

merged.sex.sce14$Pseuodtime = coldat$TSCAN.8

mplot = plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce14, dimred = "corrected", colour_by="Pseuodtime", point_size = .5) + ggline_corrected


path_plot + (fplot + mplot +  plot_layout(guides = "collect", axis_titles = "collect")) + plot_layout(widths = c(.8,2)) 
```

```{r}
pseudo_test2 <- testPseudotime(combined.sex.sce14, df=1, pseudotime=combined.sex.sce14$TSCAN.2)
```

```{r}
pseudo_test8 <- testPseudotime(combined.sex.sce14, df=1, pseudotime=combined.sex.sce14$TSCAN.8)
```

We want to find genes that are significant in path 2 and are not significant and/or changing in the opposite direction in path 8 and vise versa. We use the raw p-values to look for non-significant genes in order to increase the stringency of the definition of unique genes in our path.

## Genes uniquely changing on path 2 vs 8

These are saved in `results/no.support_sex_only2_DEgenes.csv`.

```{r}
only2 <- pseudo_test2[which(pseudo_test2$FDR <= 0.05 & 
    (pseudo_test8$p.value >= 0.05 | sign(pseudo_test2$logFC)!=sign(pseudo_test8$logFC))),]

only2 = as.data.frame(only2) 
only2$Gene = rownames(only2) 
only2 = left_join(only2, gene_description, by = "Gene")
only2 = only2[c(4,5,6,1,2,3)]

write.csv(only2, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only2_DEgenes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(only2[order(only2$p.value),], filter="top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Top 50 heatmap

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
on.path2 <- !is.na(combined.sex.sce14$TSCAN.2)

gene_description = as.data.frame(gene_description)
rownames(gene_description) = gene_description$Gene

gene_description = gene_description[rownames(combined.sex.sce14),]

rowData(combined.sex.sce14) = gene_description 
combined.sex.sce14$Pseudotime = coldat$TSCAN.2

top50 =
  only2 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(Gene) %>%
  unique()

top50_wang =
  only2 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(WangGeneID) %>%
  unique()

# -------------------------------------------
# 1. Expression matrix centered
# -------------------------------------------
mat <- logcounts(combined.sex.sce14[top50,on.path2])
rownames(mat) = top50_wang
mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat), center = TRUE))

min_f =min(mat_scaled)
max_f = max(mat_scaled)

# -------------------------------------------
# 2. Metadata + ordering by label
# -------------------------------------------
meta <- as.data.frame(colData(combined.sex.sce14[,on.path2]))
meta <- meta %>% arrange(TSCAN.2)
mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[, rownames(meta)]

# -------------------------------------------
# 3. Cluster genes
# -------------------------------------------
gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled)
gene_clust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "ward.D2")
gene_order <- gene_clust$labels[gene_clust$order]
mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_order, , drop = FALSE]

# -------------------------------------------
# 4. Long data
# -------------------------------------------
library(tibble)

df_long <- as.data.frame(mat_scaled) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Gene") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Gene, names_to = "Cell", values_to = "Expression") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("Cell"), by = "Cell")

df_long$Gene <- factor(df_long$Gene, levels = gene_order)
meta$Cell <- as.character(rownames(meta))

# -------------------------------------------
# 6. Annotation bar
# -------------------------------------------

meta$Cell <- factor(meta$Cell, levels = meta$Cell)

f_anno_bar <- ggplot(meta, aes(x = Cell, y = 1, fill = Pseudotime)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", 
                       na.value = "white",
                       guide = "none")  +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 40)
  ) + labs(fill = "Pseudotime")

# -------------------------------------------
# 7. Heatmap
# -------------------------------------------
df_long$Cell <- factor(df_long$Cell, levels = meta$Cell)

f_heatmap_plot <- 
  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Cell, y = Gene, fill = Expression)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "steelblue3", 
                      mid =  "#FFFFE0", 
                      high = "firebrick2",
                      midpoint = 0, 
                      na.value = "white",
                      limits = c(-4,5),
                      guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7, 
                               color = "black",
                               face = "italic",
                               family = "sans"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 40),
  ) + labs(fill = "Scaled\nexpression")

# -------------------------------------------
# 9. Combine
# -------------------------------------------
f_anno_bar / f_heatmap_plot + plot_layout(heights = c(0.025, 1), guides = "collect")  
# ggsave(paste0(results_dir, "/test.png"), 
#         height = 9,
#         width = 6)
```

## Gene ontology enrichment of genes uniquely changing on path 2 vs 8

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Path`: Path and whether the gene is up or down regulated      
* `nPathDEGenes`: Number of genes differentially expressed on the path         
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and path DE genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons            
          
These results are save in `results/no.support_sex_only2_gene_GO_pvals.csv`. Overlapping genes are save in `results/no.support_sex_only2_gene_GO_overlaps.csv`

```{r}
gaf = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_gene_ontology.gaf", header=F, comment = "!")

gaf <- 
  gaf %>%
  filter(!grepl("NOT", V4)) %>%  
  dplyr::select(V3, V5) %>%      
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "V5",            
                Gene = "V3") %>%
  distinct() 

keep_go = 
  gaf %>%
  group_by(GOID) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n > 10,
         n < 200) %>%
  pull(GOID)

gaf = 
  gaf %>%
  filter(GOID %in% keep_go)

go_names = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GOs_names.txt", header = F)
colnames(go_names) = c("GOID", "GOName")

only2_genes = 
  only2 %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ "Only2.Upregulated",
    logFC < 0 ~  "Only2.Downregulated")
    ) %>%
  select(Gene, Path)
  
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           only2_genes, length(merged.sex.sce))

shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SharedGene = "Gene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only2_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nPathDEGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(nSharedGenes > 0)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only2_gene_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores %>% arrange(desc(nSharedGenes)), filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Genes uniquely changing on path 8 vs 2

These are saved in `results/no.support_sex_only8_DEgenes.csv`.

```{r}
only8 <- pseudo_test8[which(pseudo_test8$FDR <= 0.05 & 
    (pseudo_test2$p.value >= 0.05 | sign(pseudo_test2$logFC)!=sign(pseudo_test8$logFC))),]

only8 = as.data.frame(only8) 
only8$Gene = rownames(only8) 
only8 = left_join(only8, gene_description, by = "Gene")
only8 = only8[c(4,5,6,1,2,3)]

write.csv(only8, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only8_DEgenes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(only8[order(only8$p.value),], filter="top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Top 50 heatmap

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
on.path8 <- !is.na(combined.sex.sce14$TSCAN.8)

gene_description = as.data.frame(gene_description)
rownames(gene_description) = gene_description$Gene

gene_description = gene_description[rownames(combined.sex.sce14),]

rowData(combined.sex.sce14) = gene_description 
combined.sex.sce14$Pseudotime = coldat$TSCAN.8

top50 =
  only8 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(Gene) %>%
  unique()

top50_wang =
  only8 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(WangGeneID) %>%
  unique()

# -------------------------------------------
# 1. Expression matrix centered
# -------------------------------------------
mat <- logcounts(combined.sex.sce14[top50,on.path8])
rownames(mat) = top50_wang
mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat), center = TRUE))

min_m = min(mat_scaled)
max_m = max(mat_scaled)

# -------------------------------------------
# 2. Metadata + ordering by label
# -------------------------------------------
meta <- as.data.frame(colData(combined.sex.sce14[,on.path8]))
meta <- meta %>% arrange(TSCAN.8)
mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[, rownames(meta)]

# -------------------------------------------
# 3. Cluster genes
# -------------------------------------------
gene_dist <- dist(mat_scaled)
gene_clust <- hclust(gene_dist, method = "ward.D2")
gene_order <- gene_clust$labels[gene_clust$order]
mat_scaled <- mat_scaled[gene_order, , drop = FALSE]

# -------------------------------------------
# 4. Long data
# -------------------------------------------
library(tibble)

df_long <- as.data.frame(mat_scaled) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Gene") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Gene, names_to = "Cell", values_to = "Expression") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("Cell"), by = "Cell")

df_long$Gene <- factor(df_long$Gene, levels = gene_order)
meta$Cell <- as.character(rownames(meta))

# -------------------------------------------
# 6. Annotation bar
# -------------------------------------------

meta$Cell <- factor(meta$Cell, levels = meta$Cell)

m_anno_bar <- ggplot(meta, aes(x = Cell, y = 1, fill = Pseudotime)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", 
                       na.value = "white")  +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 40)
  ) + labs(fill = "Pseudotime")

# -------------------------------------------
# 7. Heatmap
# -------------------------------------------
df_long$Cell <- factor(df_long$Cell, levels = meta$Cell)

m_heatmap_plot <- 
  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Cell, y = Gene, fill = Expression)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "steelblue3", 
                      mid =  "#FFFFE0", 
                      high = "firebrick2",
                      midpoint = 0, 
                      na.value = "white",
                      limits = c(-4,5)) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7, 
                               color = "black",
                               face = "italic",
                               family = "sans"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 40),
  ) + labs(fill = "Scaled\nexpression")

# -------------------------------------------
# 9. Combine
# -------------------------------------------
m_anno_bar / m_heatmap_plot + plot_layout(heights = c(0.025, 1), guides = "collect")  
# ggsave(paste0(results_dir, "/test.png"), 
#         height = 9,
#         width = 6)
```

## Gene ontology enrichment of genes uniquely changing on path 8 vs 2

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Path`: Path and whether the gene is up or down regulated      
* `nPathDEGenes`: Number of genes differentially expressed on the path         
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and path DE genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons            

These results are save in `results/no.support_sex_only8_gene_GO_pvals.csv`. Overlapping genes are save in `results/no.support_sex_only8_gene_GO_overlaps.csv`

```{r}
only8_genes = 
  only8 %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ "Only8.Upregulated",
    logFC < 0 ~  "Only8.Downregulated")
    ) %>%
  select(Gene, Path)
  
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           only8_genes, length(merged.sex.sce))


shared_gene_description = 
  gene_description %>%
  dplyr::rename(SharedGene = "Gene")

go_ol$intersecting.genes = 
  go_ol$intersecting.genes %>%
  left_join(shared_gene_description, by="SharedGene")

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only8_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nOnly8DEGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(nSharedGenes > 0)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/no.support_sex_only8_gene_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores %>% arrange(desc(nSharedGenes)), filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

# Figures

```{r}
## Add cluster annotations
#All Cells
anno_all = c("1. Interstitial stromal cells",
             "2. Mesonephric stromal cells",
             "3. Granulosa cells",
             "4. Leydig cells",
             "5. Primordial germ cells/oogonia",
             "6. Endothelial cells",
             "7. Mesonephric cells",
             "8. Erythrocytes",
             "9. Sertoli cells",
             "10. B lymphocytes",
             "11. Hemopoietic stem cells",
             "12. Mesonephric podocytes",
             "13. Macrophages",
             "14. Proliferating pre-granulosa cells",
             "15. Proliferating B cells",
             "16. Meiotic spermatocyte",
             "17. Spermatocyte",
             "18. Mitotic spermatogonia ",
             "19. Migrating male germ cells")

ct = anno_all[colData(merged.sce)$label]
merged.sce$CellType = ct
merged.sce$CellType = factor(merged.sce$CellType, levels= anno_all)

combined.sce$CellType = ct
combined.sce$CellType = factor(combined.sce$CellType, levels= anno_all)

scatter.clusters = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sce)) 
scatter.clusters$barcode = rownames(scatter.clusters)
 
scatter.clusters =
  scatter.clusters %>%
  select(barcode, label) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

scatter.clusters = scatter.clusters[colnames(combined.sce)]
combined.sce$label = scatter.clusters

```

```{r}
# Sex cells
anno =
  c("1. oogonia",
    "2. oogonia",
    "3. oogonia",
    "4. meiotic spermatocyte",
    "5. spermatocyte",
    "6. migrating male GC/spermatocyte",
    "7. mitotic spermatogonia",
    "8. migrating male GC/spermatocyte",
    "9. spermatocyte",
    "10. spermatocyte/mitotic spermatogonia",
    "11. mitotic spermatogonia",
    "12. spermatocyte",
    "13. migrating GC cells",
    "14. PCG/spermatocyte",
    "15. PCG"
    )
  

ct = anno[colData(merged.sex.sce)$label]
merged.sex.sce$CellType = ct
merged.sex.sce$CellType = factor(merged.sex.sce$CellType, levels= anno)

combined.sex.sce$CellType = ct
combined.sex.sce$CellType = factor(combined.sex.sce$CellType, levels= anno)

scatter.clusters = as.data.frame(colData(merged.sex.sce)) 
scatter.clusters$barcode = rownames(scatter.clusters)
 
scatter.clusters =
  scatter.clusters %>%
  select(barcode, label) %>%
  tibble::deframe()

scatter.clusters = scatter.clusters[colnames(combined.sex.sce)]
combined.sex.sce$label = scatter.clusters

```

```{r}
goi_long = c("BOLL", "LOC116955069", "CHEK2", "DDX4",
              "LOC116946948","LOC116952239","HSD11B1L",
              "LOC116944806","LOC116945518", "LOC116952716","NANOS1",
              "PAX2","PAX7","LOC116939357",
              "PIWIL1","POU2F2","LOC116944884","SFRP2",
              "LOC103091769","TRIP4","LOC116955055",
              "LOC116950388","CFAP69","LOC103091770",
              "LOC116948775","LOC103091860","LOC103091810",
              "LOC116940926","LOC116948663","KDM1A",
              "PEG10","SCFD1","SPAG5","SPAG16",
              "SPATA22","TEX11","LOC116950791","WNT11",
              "ZAR1L")
```

## Supplemental violin plots

### Violin plots -- all cells
```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10, eval=FALSE}
# Violin plots -- all cells

combined.sce.plot = combined.sce[goi_long,]

goi_names = 
  gene_description %>%
  filter(Gene %in% goi_long) %>%
  pull(WangGeneID)

rownames(combined.sce.plot) = goi_names

goi_names_alph = goi_names[order(goi_names)]

plot_config <- data.frame(
  start = c(1, 8, 15, 22, 28, 36),
  end = c(7, 14, 21, 29, 35, 39),
  height = c(10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 6)
)

for (i in 1:nrow(plot_config)) {
  p <- scater::plotExpression(combined.sce.plot, 
                              x = "label", 
                              colour_by = "CellType",
                              features = goi_names_alph[plot_config$start[i]:plot_config$end[i]], 
                              assay.type = "logcounts",
                              scales = "free_y",
                              ncol = 1,
                              point_size = .5,
                              scattermore = TRUE) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6)) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(results_dir, "/all_cells_vln", i, ".png"), 
         plot = p,
         width = 8, 
         height = plot_config$height[i])
}

```

### Violin plots -- sex cells
```{r, fig.height=10, fig.width=10, eval=FALSE}
# Violin plots -- sex cells
combined.sex.sce.plot = combined.sex.sce[goi_long,]

goi_names = 
  gene_description %>%
  filter(Gene %in% goi_long) %>%
  select(Gene, WangGeneID) %>%
  deframe()

goi_names = goi_names[goi_long]

rownames(combined.sex.sce.plot) = goi_names

goi_names_alph = goi_names[order(goi_names)]

for (i in 1:nrow(plot_config)) {
  p <- scater::plotExpression(combined.sex.sce.plot, 
                              x = "label", 
                              colour_by = "CellType",
                              features = goi_names_alph[plot_config$start[i]:plot_config$end[i]], 
                              assay.type = "logcounts",
                              scales = "free_y",
                              ncol = 1,
                              point_size = .5,
                              scattermore = TRUE) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6)) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)))
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(results_dir, "/sex_cells_vln", i, ".png"), 
         plot = p,
         width = 8, 
         height = plot_config$height[i])
}

```

## Figure 1. Cell type identification in lamprey gonadal single-cell RNA-seq data.

```{r}
all_tsne = plotTSNE(merged.sce, colour_by="CellType", point_size = .05) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=5)))
```

```{r}
library(tibble)
goi_short_wang = c("BOLL", "CSF1R", "CHEK2", "DDX4",
                   "FOXL2","GATA2-3","HSD11B1L",
                   "LHX8","LYVE1", "LYZ","NANOS1",
                   "PAX2","PAX7","PDGFRA",
                   "PIWIL1","POU2F2","PTPRC","SFRP2",
                   "sox9-a","TRIP4","FLT1-1",
                   "WNT4")

goi_short = 
  gene_description %>%
  filter(WangGeneID %in% goi_short_wang) %>%
  select(WangGeneID, Gene) %>%
  deframe()
```

```{r}
# bubble
sce = combined.sce
goi = goi_long

sce.plot = sce[goi,]

goi_names = 
  gene_description %>%
  filter(Gene %in% goi) %>%
  select(Gene, WangGeneID) %>%
  deframe()

goi_names = goi_names[goi]

rownames(sce.plot) = goi_names

mat <- logcounts(sce.plot)
mat = as.matrix(mat)

meta <- as.data.frame(colData(sce.plot))
meta <- meta %>% arrange(label)

df_long <- as.data.frame(mat) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Gene") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Gene, names_to = "Cell", values_to = "Expression") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("Cell"), by = "Cell") %>%
  select(Gene, Cell, CellType, Expression)

markers = read.csv(paste0(results_dir, "/scatter_cluster_marker_genes.csv"), row.names = 1)

cluster_index = as.numeric(gsub("cluster_","",markers$cluster))
markers$CellType = anno_all[cluster_index]

markers = 
  markers %>%
  select(WangGeneID, CellType, FDR, summary.logFC) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Gene = "WangGeneID")

plot_data <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Gene, CellType) %>%
  summarize(
    percent_expressed = mean(Expression > 0) * 100,
    avg_expression = mean(Expression[Expression > 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(Gene) %>%
  mutate(
    scaled_expression = scale(avg_expression)[,1]  # Z-score by gene
```
